title: Metal working production and maintenance fitters and technicians
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 119 related US occupations.)

work_activities.element_name: Communicating with Supervisors, Peers, or Subordinates
Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards
Judging the Qualities of Objects, Services, or People
Estimating the Quantifiable Characteristics of Products, Events, or Information
Operating Vehicles, Mechanized Devices, or Equipment
Interpreting the Meaning of Information for Others
Coordinating the Work and Activities of Others
Communicating with People Outside the Organization
Drafting, Laying Out, and Specifying Technical Devices, Parts, and Equipment
Providing Consultation and Advice to Others
Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others
Performing for or Working Directly with the Public

work_context.element_name: Face-to-Face Discussions with Individuals and Within Teams
Work With or Contribute to a Work Group or Team
Deal With External Customers or the Public in General
Coordinate or Lead Others in Accomplishing Work Activities
Health and Safety of Other Workers
Work Outcomes and Results of Other Workers
Dealing With Unpleasant, Angry, or Discourteous People
Dealing with Violent or Physically Aggressive People
Outdoors, Exposed to All Weather Conditions
In an Open Vehicle or Operating Equipment
In an Enclosed Vehicle or Operate Enclosed Equipment
Exposed to Sounds, Noise Levels that are Distracting or Uncomfortable
Exposed to Very Hot or Cold Temperatures
Exposed to Extremely Bright or Inadequate Lighting Conditions
Exposed to Cramped Work Space, Awkward Positions
Exposed to Minor Burns, Cuts, Bites, or Stings
Spend Time Climbing Ladders, Scaffolds, or Poles
Spend Time Kneeling, Crouching, Stooping, or Crawling
Spend Time Keeping or Regaining Balance
Spend Time Using Your Hands to Handle, Control, or Feel Objects, Tools, or Controls
Spend Time Bending or Twisting Your Body
Wear Common Protective or Safety Equipment such as Safety Shoes, Glasses, Gloves, Hearing Protection, Hard Hats, or Life Jackets
Wear Specialized Protective or Safety Equipment such as Breathing Apparatus, Safety Harness, Full Protection Suits, or Radiation Protection
Impact of Decisions on Co-workers or Company Results
Importance of Being Exact or Accurate
Pace Determined by Speed of Equipment

tasks.task: Instruct other pilots and student pilots in aircraft operations and the principles of flight, and teach company regulations and procedures to other pilots.

Coordinate flight activities with ground crews and air traffic control, inform crew members of flight and test procedures, and file instrument flight plans with air traffic control to ensure flights are coordinated with other air traffic.
Use instrumentation to guide flights when visibility is poor and monitor engine operation, fuel consumption, and functioning of aircraft systems during flights.
Respond to and report in-flight emergencies and malfunctions, and check aircraft prior to flights to ensure that the engines, controls, instruments, and other systems are functioning properly.

Choose routes, altitudes, and speeds that will provide the fastest, safest, and smoothest flights, and obtain and review data like load weights and weather conditions to determine flight plans and identify needed changes.
Record flight information, including flight times, altitudes flown, and fuel consumption, in flight records and log books.
Conduct in-flight tests and evaluations at specified altitudes and in various weather conditions to determine the receptivity and other characteristics of equipment and systems.
Supervise other crew members and direct activities of aircraft crews during flights.
Perform minor aircraft maintenance and repair work or arrange for major maintenance, and install and adjust speed and gear mechanisms, brakes, and brake pads.
Fly with other pilots or pilot-license applicants to evaluate their proficiency and monitor progress of environmental improvement programs.
Make announcements regarding flights and contact control towers for takeoff clearances and arrival instructions, using radio equipment.
Check baggage or cargo to ensure that it has been loaded correctly and check passenger and cargo distributions and fuel amounts to meet weight and balance specifications.

Plan flights according to government and company regulations, using aeronautical charts and navigation instruments, and steer aircraft along planned routes, using autopilot and flight management computers.
Order changes in fuel supplies, loads, routes, or schedules to ensure safety of flights.
Confer with flight dispatchers and weather forecasters to stay informed of flight conditions, and evaluate other pilots or pilot-license applicants for proficiency.
Perform general marine vessel maintenance or repair work, or assist other crew members with maintenance or repair duties.
Read and interpret engineering drawings, diagrams, instructions, or specifications to determine needed repairs and fabrication methods.
Test, evaluate, and classify excess or in-use medical equipment to determine serviceability and condition, and report issues based on regulations.
Maintain or repair engines, mechanical or electrical equipment, and assist in the supervision of repair activities.
Requisition parts or materials and research catalogs or repair part lists to locate sources for repair parts.
Adjust, calibrate, and verify the accuracy of equipment such as cameras, light meters, and monitoring system components, using appropriate tools and timing instruments.
Disassemble equipment to access defects, using hand tools; repair or replace defective parts.
Install and connect components of systems, such as solar collectors, plumbing installations, pipe fittings, and medical equipment, in accordance with specifications and using hand or power tools.
Perform regular maintenance, including cleaning, lubricating, and adjusting equipment, as well as conducting routine inspections for hazards and defects.
Fabricate and assemble parts using machinery to ensure compliance with specifications, including tasks such as cutting, threading, welding, or soldering components.
Inspect, test, and troubleshoot various systems or equipment, ensuring compliance with operational standards and safety regulations.
Conduct research and development to improve processes and systems, including testing proposed processes in laboratory settings or pilot plants.
Plan and layout pipe systems and related equipment installation, assessing structural integrity and the spatial arrangement of operations.
Connect and install various plumbing, heating, and cooling systems, ensuring proper flow and conducting quality checks for leak detection and system integrity.
Establish safety procedures for the operation of equipment and the handling of materials to minimize hazards in the workplace.
Estimate production costs and progress, and maintain records for management review, detailing labor, materials, and timelines.
Train or supervise assistants in procedural tasks such as pipe installation and assembly, ensuring safety and operational efficiency.
Maintain and lubricate mechanical components or assist with repairs on timepieces or other intricate devices, using specialized tools.
Modify existing systems for enhanced efficiency, such as upgrading equipment or incorporating sustainable technologies like alternative energy sources.
Monitor and analyze performance data from engineering processes and experiments to inform adjustments and improvements.
Align and position components or subassemblies during assembly, ensuring accuracy and adherence to design specifications.
Document service records and repair histories for equipment and devices to ensure proper maintenance and compliance with regulations.
Train or supervise others in specialized techniques related to mathematical applications or technical drawing.
Document inspection and test results and work performed or to be performed.
Start motors and monitor performance for signs of malfunctioning, such as smoke, excessive vibration, or misfiring.
Mount motors to boats and operate boats at various speeds on waterways to conduct operational tests.
Disassemble and inspect motors to locate defective parts using mechanic's hand tools and gauges.
Seal open sides of modular units to prepare them for shipment using polyethylene sheets, nails, and hammers.
Reset hardware using chisels, mallets, and screwdrivers.
Refinish wood surfaces on cabinets, doors, moldings, or floors using power sanders, putty, spray equipment, brushes, paints, or varnishes.
Repair leaks with caulking compound or replace pipes using pipe wrenches.
Remove damaged exterior panels and repair and replace structural frame members.
Drive machines into position at working faces.
Connect water hoses to inlet pipes of plumbing systems, and test operation of toilets or sinks.
Connect electrical systems to outside power sources and activate switches to test the operation of appliances or light fixtures.
List parts needed, estimate costs, and plan work procedures using parts lists, technical manuals, or diagrams.
Mark dimensions or outlines on stone prior to cutting using rules and chalk lines.
Examine or test operation of parts or systems to ensure completeness of repairs.
Repair plumbing or propane gas lines using caulking compounds and plastic or copper pipe.
Inspect recreational vehicles to diagnose problems and perform necessary adjustment, repair, or overhaul.
Dismantle and store rigging equipment after use.
Attach pulleys and blocks to fixed overhead structures using bolts and clamps.
Manipulate rigging lines, hoists, and pulling gear to move or support materials such as heavy equipment, ships, or theatrical sets.
Observe and listen to equipment operation to detect binding or stoppage of tools or other equipment malfunctions.
Repair, oil, and adjust machines, and change cutting teeth, using wrenches.
Inspect and verify dimensions and clearances of parts to ensure conformance to factory specifications.
Inspect, repair, and maintain automotive and mechanical equipment and machinery such as pumps and compressors.
Disassemble and overhaul internal combustion engines, pumps, generators, transmissions, clutches, and differential units.
Remove damaged sections of vehicles using metal-cutting guns, air grinders, and wrenches, and install replacement parts using wrenches or welding equipment.
Repair engine mechanical equipment such as power tilts, bilge pumps, or power take-offs.
Inspect, repair, or replace brake systems.
Diagnose and repair vehicle heating and cooling systems.
Inspect repaired vehicles for proper functioning and completion of work, and test-drive vehicles to ensure proper alignment and handling.
Perform routine maintenance such as changing oil, checking batteries, and lubricating equipment and machinery.
Use hand tools and power tools such as pneumatic wrenches, lathes, welding equipment, or jacks and hoists.
Perform routine and scheduled maintenance services, such as oil changes, lubrications, and tune-ups.
Test drive vehicles and test components and systems using equipment such as infrared engine analyzers, compression gauges, and computerized diagnostic devices.
Review damage reports, prepare or review repair cost estimates, and plan work to be performed.
Confer with customers to obtain descriptions of vehicle problems and to discuss work to be performed and future repair requirements.
Assist mechanics and perform various mechanical duties such as changing oil or checking and replacing batteries.
Inspect mining areas for unsafe structures, equipment, and working conditions.
Remove damaged panels and identify the family and properties of the plastic used on a vehicle.
Repair, reline, replace, and adjust brakes.
Align, level, and anchor machinery.

Obtain windshields or windows for specific automobile makes and models from stock and examine them for defects prior to installation. Allow all glass parts installed with urethane ample time to cure, taking temperature and humidity into account. Remove all dirt, foreign matter, and loose glass from damaged areas, apply primer along windshield or window edges, and allow primer to dry. Maintain or repair vehicles with alternative fuel systems, including biodiesel, hybrid, or compressed natural gas vehicles. Repair or adjust seats, doors, or windows. Remove broken or damaged glass windshields or window glass from motor vehicles, using hand tools to remove screws from frames holding glass. Install replacement glass in vehicles. Replace all moldings, clips, windshield wipers, or other parts that were removed prior to glass replacement or repair. Install, repair, or replace safety glass and related materials, such as back glass heating elements, on vehicles or equipment. Remove moldings, clips, windshield wipers, screws, bolts, and inside A-pillar moldings and lower headliners in preparation for installation or repair work. Install, level, and align machine components such as gears, chains, guides, dies, cutters, or needles to set up machinery for operation. Inspect products to ensure that specifications are met and to determine if machines need adjustment. Start machines, monitor operations, and make adjustments as needed. Perform routine maintenance and upgrade work on machines and equipment, such as replacing parts, building up drill bits, and lubricating machinery. Perform quality checks on products and parts. Circular tasks involving cooperation with coworkers to obtain information about orders, processes, or problems.

Apply new technologies developed to minimize the environmental impact of coal mining. Follow green operational practices involving conservation of water or energy or reduction of solid waste. Evaluate data to develop new mining products, equipment, or processes. Test air to detect toxic gases and recommend measures to remove them, such as installation of ventilation shafts. Implement and coordinate mine safety programs, including the design and maintenance of protective and rescue equipment and safety devices. Design, develop, and implement computer applications for use in mining operations such as mine design, modeling, or mapping or for monitoring mine conditions. Conduct engineering research experiments to improve or modify mining and oil machinery and operations. Coordinate activities of workers engaged in research, planning, and development. Assist engineering and other personnel to solve operating problems.

Remove drill bits from chucks after drilling holes, and insert bolts into chucks. Confer with customers, read work orders, or examine vehicles needing repair to determine the nature and extent of damage. Dismantle engines and repair or replace defective parts, such as magnetos, carburetors, or generators. Reassemble and test subassembly units. Select appropriate tools, safety equipment, and parts, according to job requirements. Unbolt and remove wheels from vehicles, using lug wrenches or other hand or power tools. Tighten ends of anchored truss bolts, using turnbuckles. Rotate chucks to turn bolts and open expansion heads against rock formations. Position bolting machines, and insert drill bits into chucks. Cool or warm glass in the event of temperature extremes. Replace or adjust motorized or manual window-raising mechanisms. Hold cut or uneven edges of glass against automated abrasive belts to shape or smooth edges. Thread yarn, thread, and fabric through guides, needles, and rollers of machines for weaving, knitting, or other processing. Examine looms to determine causes of loom stoppage, such as warp filling, harness breaks, or mechanical defects. Assemble and position machines, augers, casing pipes, and other equipment, using hand and power tools.

Drill bolt holes into roofs at specified distances from ribs or adjacent bolts. Maintain records of drilling and production operations. Develop plans for oil and gas field drilling, and for product recovery and treatment. Analyze data to recommend placement of wells and supplementary processes to enhance production. Monitor production rates, and plan rework processes to improve production. Assess costs and estimate the production capabilities and economic value of oil and gas wells, to evaluate the economic viability of potential drilling sites. Supervise the removal of drilling equipment, the removal of any waste, and the safe return of land to structural stability when wells or pockets are exhausted. Signal crane operators to move equipment. Start, stop, and control drilling speed of machines and insertion of casings into holes. Drive or guide truck-mounted equipment into position, level and stabilize rigs, and extend telescoping derricks. Retrieve lost equipment from bore holes, using retrieval tools and equipment. Measure depths of drilled blast holes, using weighted tape measures.

Inspect machinery to determine whether repairs are needed. Notify supervisors or repair staff of mechanical malfunctions. Confer with scientific, engineering, and technical personnel to resolve design, research, and testing problems. Monitor mine production rates to assess operational effectiveness. Prepare schedules, reports, and estimates of the costs involved in developing and operating mines. Write technical reports for engineering and management personnel. Perform engineering research experiments to improve or modify mining and oil machinery and operations. Study guides, loom patterns, samples, charts, or specification sheets, or confer with supervisors or engineering staff to determine setup requirements.

Scrape or wash conveyors, using belt scrapers or belt washers, to minimize dust production. Lower perforating guns into wells, using hoists; then use measuring devices and instrument panels to position guns in correct positions for taking samples. Coordinate the installation, maintenance, and operation of mining and oil field equipment. Clean, gauge, and lubricate gun ports. Supervise assemblers and train employees on job procedures. Ensure proper adjustments and corrections are made following inspections. Complete production reports to communicate team production level to management. Record information about work completed and machine settings. Package finished products and prepare them for shipment. Move and store inventories of explosives, loaded perforating guns, and other materials, according to established safety procedures. Compile and keep gun and explosives records in compliance with local and federal laws. Design well pumping systems. Disinfect, reconstruct, and redevelop contaminated wells and water pumping systems, and clean and disinfect new wells in preparation for use.

Remove defects in cloth by cutting and pulling out filling. Adjust machine heating mechanisms, tensions, and speeds to produce specified products. Hammer out dents and bends in frames and weld tears and breaks. Drive trucks, tractors, or truck-mounted drills to and from work sites. Install new foam dams on pinchwelds, if required. Test machinery and equipment to ensure that it is safe and conforms to performance specifications. Select or devise materials-handling methods and equipment to transport ore, waste materials, and mineral products efficiently and economically. Coordinate the installation, maintenance, and operation of mining and oil field equipment.

Verify detonation of charges by observing control panels or by listening for the sounds of blasts, and examine blast areas to determine amounts and kinds of explosive charges needed while ensuring that safety laws are observed.
Light fuses, drop detonating devices into wells or boreholes, or activate firing devices with plungers, dials, or buttons to set off single or multiple blasts.
Insert, pack, and pour explosives such as dynamite, ammonium nitrate, and slurries into blast holes; then shovel drill cuttings, admit water into boreholes, and tamp material to compact charges.
Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites, and maintain inventory levels by ordering new supplies as necessary.
Cut specified lengths of primacord and attach primers to cord ends, and tie specified lengths of delaying fuses into patterns for timing sequences of explosions.

Document geological formations encountered during work and place explosive charges in holes or other spots; then detonate explosives to demolish structures or to loosen, remove, or displace materials.
Mark patterns, locations, and depths of charge holes for drilling, and issue drilling instructions to ensure safe and effective operations.

Set up and operate short-wave radio or field telephone equipment to transmit and receive blast information, and review client requirements and proposed locations for drilling operations to determine feasibility and cost estimates.
Withdraw drill rods from holes and extract core samples to inspect or take samples to laboratories for analysis and to determine the nature of strata.
Maintain stocks of parts used in on-site installation, maintenance, and repair of appliances, and provide repair cost estimates, recommending whether repairs or replacements are better.
Record maintenance and repair work performed on appliances, and disassemble appliances to diagnose and repair problems.
Replace worn and defective parts such as switches, bearings, or circuit boards, and service or repair domestic electrical or gas appliances, such as stoves and refrigerators.

Talk to customers or refer to work orders to establish the nature of appliance malfunctions, and inspect wiring connections, control panel hookups, and alignments to ensure proper equipment operation.
Test and repair electrical circuits and sensors, following wiring and system specifications, and verify that safety regulations and building codes are met.
Install, maintain, or repair security systems and related equipment following blueprints, and mount and fasten components, including electrical and telephone wiring.
Read specifications or blueprints to determine locations and sizes of required materials, and inspect and test equipment to locate damage or worn parts, diagnosing malfunctions accordingly.
Assemble, install, repair, and maintain elevators, escalators, and moving sidewalks using hand and power tools, and ensure all components operate correctly through testing and inspection.
Install electrical wires and controls by attaching conduit along shaft walls from floor to floor and pulling plastic-covered wires through the conduit.
Measure and adjust settings on systems for optimal performance, using programming keypads, and conduct regular maintenance and inspections to confirm functionality and safety.
Order replacement parts as necessary, and maintain logs detailing all repairs and checks performed.
Inspector and repair mechanical control devices or valves using power tools, and reassemble them to specified requirements.
Repair and rebuild defective mechanical parts in electric motors, generators, and related equipment using hand and power tools.
Lift units or parts such as motors or generators, using cranes or chain hoists, and signal crane operators to lift heavy parts or subassemblies.
Keep informed of new products and developments, and participate in additional training to keep skills up to date.
Inspect equipment and structural steel members to verify vertical and horizontal alignment using plumb bobs, laser equipment, and levels.
Fabricate metal parts according to blueprints or supervisors' instructions, including cutting, bending, or welding operations.
Disassemble defective units and repair or replace parts, including locks, gears, cables, and wiring as necessary.
Connect electrical wiring to control panels and electric motors, ensuring that all components meet specifications and safety requirements.
Dismantle structures or equipment as needed, and ensure all necessary safety procedures are followed during repairs and installations.
Inspect electrical connections and wiring relays to ensure compliance with wiring diagrams and rectify any detected issues during testing.
Clean and reinstall parts, as well as clean internal compartments and moving parts using rags and cleaning compounds.
Observe and test operation of appliances following installation, making any necessary initial adjustments.
Run low voltage wiring on ceiling surfaces using insulated staples.
Study blueprints and schematic diagrams to determine appropriate methods of installing or repairing automated door openers and other systems.
Operate lifts, winches, or chain falls to move heavy items into position and install components using hand tools and power tools.
Fabricate replacements for worn or broken parts using welders, lathes, and other machinery, and align and fit parts according to specifications using appropriate tools.
Test and repair systems and components, including track circuits, appliances, and other electrical or mechanical systems to ensure proper functioning.
Install and adjust various door mechanisms and appliances, ensuring they operate correctly and meet specifications.
Collect payment upon job completion and complete required paperwork such as work orders.
Inspect and maintain equipment to ensure compliance with safety standards, and perform regular safety checks.
Inspect various installations and repaired systems to ensure they conform to engineering standards and specifications, providing technical advice as necessary.
Participate in assembly of new or reconditioned appliances, engaging in minor carpentry work as needed for proper installation.
Test and ensure compliance of installations with environmental regulations and standards, evaluating materials for conformance.
Observe site conditions and project compliance, documenting progress and ensuring tasks align with designs and safety standards.
Provide instruction and assistance to customers regarding appliance operation and service contacts.
Evaluate and manage modifications for existing systems or projects to enhance performance or safety.
Lift, move, or install large materials, using appropriate lifting equipment and techniques to ensure safety and accuracy.
Lubricate moving parts on various systems, ensuring smooth operation during use.
Design, fabricate and assemble components necessary for installation, ensuring systems meet functional requirements.
Conduct pressure tests on systems to ensure integrity and operational safety.
Perform experiments and modeling to conduct research on materials like metals and their properties as related to structural integrity and efficiency.

Operate machinery to move materials, such as coal, rock, or ore, and conduct maintenance or repair of transportation systems. Drive machines into piles of material and move trailing electrical cables clear of obstructions.

Diagnose or repair problems with electronic equipment, such as sound, navigation, communication, and security equipment, in motor vehicles; testing and analyzing performance using test equipment or software and applying knowledge of the functional operation of electronic units and systems; confer with customers to determine the nature of malfunctions.
Install equipment and accessories, such as stereos, navigation equipment, communication equipment, security systems, and alarm and electronic access systems.
Estimate costs of repairs, based on parts and labor charges, and evaluate tool drawing designs by measuring drawing dimensions, comparing with original specifications for form and function.
Run new speaker and electrical cables; cut openings and drill holes for fixtures and equipment, using electric drills and routers.

Replace and clean electrical or electronic components; remove seats, carpeting, and interiors of doors and add sound-absorbing material in empty spaces, reinstalling interior parts; record results of diagnostic tests and keep logs of equipment performance problems, repairs, calibrations, or tests.

Build fiberglass or wooden enclosures for sound components and fit them to automobile dimensions; prepare specifications, designs, or sketches for machines, components, or systems related to mechanical or fluid energy.

Perform or execute manual or automated tests of automotive system or component performance, efficiency, or durability; set up and conduct tests of complete units and components under operational conditions to investigate proposals for improving equipment performance; analyze test results in relation to design or rated specifications and test objectives, and modify or adjust equipment to meet specifications; record test procedures and results, numerical and graphical data, and recommendations for changes in product or test methods.

Review project instructions and blueprints to ascertain test specifications, procedures, and objectives; identify, modify and plan requirements for fabrication, assembly and testing; study blueprints, sketches, models, or specifications to plan sequences of operations for fabricating tools, dies, or assemblies.

Confer with engineering personnel regarding details and implications of test procedures and results; provide technical support to other employees regarding mechanical design, fabrication, testing, or documentation.

Assemble or disassemble complex mechanical systems; align, fit, or assemble components using hand tools, power tools, fixtures, templates, or microscopes; install or program computer hardware or machine or instrumentation software in microprocessor-based systems; fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or assemblies.

Inspect components of industrial equipment for accurate assembly and installation or for defects; perform scheduled preventive maintenance tasks, such as checking, cleaning, or repairing equipment to detect and prevent problems; test machines, components, materials, or products to determine characteristics such as performance, strength, or response to stress.

Repair or adjust equipment, machines, or defective components, replacing worn parts; modify, maintain, or repair electrical, electronic or mechanical components, equipment, or systems to ensure proper functioning, including the calibration of testing instruments; make repairs to robots or peripheral equipment, such as replacement of defective circuit boards or sensors.

Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions; troubleshoot robotic systems using knowledge of microprocessors, programmable controllers, electronics, circuit analysis, mechanics, sensor or feedback systems, hydraulics, or pneumatics; perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components; train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots.

Install new robotic systems in stationary positions or on tracks; program computer numerical control (CNC) machines to fabricate model parts, and design jigs, fixtures, and templates for use as work aids in the fabrication of parts or products; construct platforms, fixtures, and jigs for holding and placing patterns; keep records of company locks and keys.

Test performance of electromechanical assemblies, robotic assemblies, or industrial equipment using test instruments such as oscilloscopes, electronic voltmeters, or bridges; prepare layouts of machinery, tools, plants, or equipment.

Document robotics test procedures and results; maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems; establish and maintain inventory, records, or documentation systems; conduct statistical studies to analyze or compare production costs for sustainable and nonsustainable designs; specify, coordinate, or conduct quality-control or quality-assurance programs and procedures.

Train engineers or assist engineers in product testing through activities such as setting up instrumentation for automobile crash tests; assist in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems.

Evaluate the efficiency and reliability of industrial robotic systems, reprogramming or calibrating to achieve maximum quantity and quality; specify, coordinate, or conduct programs related to the environmental impact of engineering activities; identify energy-conserving production or fabrication methods.

Prepare written documentation of electromechanical test results; create computer models of patterns or parts, using modeling software; use computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) software or hardware to fabricate model parts.

Design and create templates, patterns, or coreboxes according to work orders, sample parts, or mockups; repair and rework templates and patterns; clean and finish patterns or templates, using emery cloths, files, scrapers, and power grinders; mark identification numbers or symbols onto patterns or templates.

Verify dimensions, alignments, and clearances of finished parts for conformance to specifications using measuring instruments such as calipers and micrometers; analyze or estimate production costs, such as labor, equipment, and plant space.

Operate equipment to demonstrate proper use or to analyze malfunctions; sign overhaul documents for equipment replaced or repaired; enter information into computer to copy program or to draw, modify, or store schematics using knowledge of software package used.

Schedule and supervise splicing or termination of cables in color-code order; maintain inventory of spare parts and consult with customers, supervisors, or engineers to plan layout of equipment or to resolve problems in system operation or maintenance.
Select and apply various materials to produce plastic patterns, including wood, resin, fiberglass, and coatings such as lacquer or sealing wax.
Develop, program, and test robotic systems, and provide training to others on their installation, use, and maintenance.
Repair, rework, or calibrate hydraulic or pneumatic assemblies or systems to meet operational specifications or tolerances.
Operate metalworking machines to fabricate housings, jigs, fittings, or fixtures and read blueprints or technical documents to determine assembly methods.
Verify part dimensions and align, fit, or assemble component parts using precision measuring instruments, hand or power tools, and fixtures.
Assemble pattern sections and mechanical, electrical, or electronic components into models or prototypes.
Program computerized numerical control machine tools and set up machines like lathes and drill presses to fabricate prototypes or models.
Inspect and test parts for surface defects and ensure conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments and circuit testers.
Document specifications, production operations, and final dimensions of models to establish operating standards and procedures.
Conduct research and develop methodologies related to nanotechnology and automated systems, including process or equipment changes.
Design or develop products or systems related to nanotechnology, robotics, and automated industrial tasks.
Create or maintain formal engineering documents, schematics, and bills of materials to support manufacturing processes.
Maintain and oversee engineering project files and schedules, ensuring effective execution of engineering tasks and teamwork among engineers.
Review specifications and recommend engineering or manufacturing changes to improve product functionality and performance.
Gather and analyze data, prepare reports, and provide technical guidance on engineering designs and processes for projects and research.
Move, position, and align machinery and equipment, ensuring they are secured and leveled according to established guidelines.
Supervise and consult on specifications and enhancements related to engineering, manufacturing, and project compliance.
Connect power unit to machines or steam piping to equipment, and test unit to evaluate its mechanical operation.

Replace defective parts of machines, adjust clearances and alignment of moving parts, and repair or replace damaged or worn parts using hand tools, milling machines, lathes, welding equipment, grinders, or saws.
Direct workers who are assembling or disassembling equipment or cleaning parts, and train end-users, distributors, installers, or other technicians in operational procedures.
Fabricate needed parts from sheet metal and other materials, and manufacture new metal parts using machine tools such as drill presses and engine lathes.
Document design processes or changes to equipment, and record details of repairs made and parts used after maintenance activities.
Build, configure, or test robotic applications and evaluate robotic systems or prototypes.
Align machines or equipment using various tools, adjust and maintain industrial machinery, and perform routine maintenance using control and regulating devices.
Dismantle machines using hand tools to repair or transport machinery, and reassemble units following repairs or maintenance.
Inspect equipment and listen for faults, read inspection reports, and confer with clients to identify malfunctions using test meters and diagnostic equipment.
Conduct routine maintenance on machinery, including cleaning, lubricating, and performing adjustments as required.
Test and replace electrical components and wiring across systems using various testing equipment, ensuring compliance with specifications after repairs.
Fit bearings and adjust, repair, or overhaul mobile mechanical, hydraulic, and pneumatic equipment.
Monitor and analyze energy consumption and collaborate on energy management projects.
Maintain, repair, and overhaul farm machinery and vehicles such as tractors and harvesters.
Analyze test results or machine error messages to diagnose equipment problems.
Overhaul and test machines or equipment to ensure operational efficiency.
Operate and inspect machines or heavy equipment to identify issues and ensure performance after repairs.
Design or develop plans for remediation of impacted environments, and create project plans for equipment or facility improvements including timelines and budgets.
Culminate energy conservation analyses and prepare energy-related reports or documentation.
Perform thermal or stress analyses on systems, and identify energy savings strategies for enhanced efficiency.
Identify materials appropriate for mechatronic system designs and research new materials to be used for products, such as implanted artificial organs.
Create mechanical models to simulate mechatronic design concepts and develop models or computer simulations of human biobehavioral systems to obtain data for measuring or controlling life processes.
Determine the feasibility, costs, or performance benefits of new mechatronic equipment and evaluate machining procedures to recommend changes or modifications for improved efficiency or adaptability.
Observe and test the operation of machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions, using voltmeters or other testing devices, and test units for operability before and after repairs.
Reassemble equipment after completion of inspections, testing, or repairs and reassemble machines after the completion of repair or maintenance work.
Examine parts for defects, such as breakage or excessive wear, and inspect components such as bearings, seals, gaskets, wheels, and coupler assemblies to determine if repairs are needed.

Repair or maintain the operating condition of industrial production or processing machinery or equipment, including troubleshooting equipment, electrical components, hydraulics, or other mechanical systems.
Repair or replace broken or malfunctioning components of machinery or equipment, using hand tools, torque wrenches, power tools, and welding equipment, including repairing bent or torn sheet metal.
Review building plans to verify compliance with fire code, and inspect buildings or building designs to determine fire protection system requirements and potential problems.
Disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts and make repairs, including dismantling units such as water pumps, control valves, and compressors.

Conduct experiments that will provide information about acceptable methods of nuclear material usage, nuclear fuel reclamation, or waste disposal, and develop plans for the prevention of destruction by fire, wind, and water.

Test experimental models under simulated operating conditions, for purposes such as development, standardization, or feasibility of design and evaluate the safety, efficiency, and effectiveness of biomedical equipment.

Conduct or direct testing of air quality, noise, temperature, or radiation levels to verify compliance with health and safety regulations and keep abreast of developments and changes in the nuclear field by reading technical journals or by independent study and research.
Provide technical advice and guidance to organizations on how to handle health-related problems, including collaborating with other workers to repair or move machines, machine parts, or equipment.
Fabricate and dismantle parts, equipment, and machines, using a cutting torch or other cutting equipment and operate equipment to verify operational efficiency.
Evaluate product designs for safety and inspect facilities, machinery, or safety equipment to identify and correct potential hazards and ensure safety regulation compliance.
record production, repair, and machine maintenance information, and maintain machine tools in proper operational condition.
Maintain and apply knowledge of current policies, regulations, and industrial processes, and consult with authorities to discuss safety regulations and to recommend changes as necessary.
Conduct preventative maintenance and repair, and lubricate machines and equipment, including installing and repairing agricultural irrigation, plumbing, and sprinkler systems.
Train operators, engineers, or other personnel on proper use of equipment, including conducting training or in-services to educate clinicians and other personnel.

Collaborate with engineering, supervisory, or manufacturing personnel to exchange technical information and confer with numerical control programmers to check and ensure that new programs or machinery will function properly and that output will meet specifications.
Develop training materials and conduct training sessions on fire protection, and compile, analyze, and interpret statistical data related to occupational illnesses and accidents.
Repair, rewire, and troubleshoot electrical systems, including replacing defective wiring and insulation, and tightening electrical connections, using hand tools.
Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products and investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures.
Devise corrective actions or order plant shutdowns in emergency situations, including advising hospital administrators on the planning, acquisition, and use of medical equipment.
Follow established safety protocols and procedures, and dispose of scrap or waste material in accordance with company policies and environmental regulations.
Determine appropriate methods for fabricating and joining materials, and design processing plants and equipment.
Monitor material performance, evaluate its deterioration, and conduct tests on raw materials or finished products to ensure quality.
Supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists, and instruct other workers in machine set-up and operation.
Design and direct the testing or control of processing procedures, and plan and implement laboratory operations to develop material and fabrication procedures.
Evaluate technical specifications and economic factors relating to process or product design objectives, and analyze project requests to determine feasibility and cost.
Perform managerial functions, such as preparing proposals and budgets, and provide technical customer service.
Solve problems in various engineering fields, and provide feedback to design engineers on customer problems or needs.
Plan and evaluate new projects, consulting with other engineers and corporate executives as necessary.
Review new product plans, making recommendations for material selection based on design objectives.
Modify properties of metal alloys using thermal and mechanical treatments, and select proper coolants and lubricants and start their flow.
Guide technical staff in developing materials for specific uses in projected products or devices.
Write programs for computer numerical control (CNC) machines to cut metal and plastic materials, and read blueprints and technical drawings to determine product specifications.
Set machine stops or guides to specified lengths and measure, cut, and install frameworks and conduit to support and connect wiring and controls.
Record operational data, and monitor the feed and speed of machines during the machining process.
Inspect workpieces for defects and measure workpieces to determine accuracy of machine operation.
Machine parts to specifications using machine tools, and start machines and engage feeding, cooling, and lubricating mechanisms.
Inspect and test electrical systems and equipment to locate and diagnose malfunctions, based on schematics and manufacturer's specifications.
Conduct research to analyze feasibility, design, operation, or performance of equipment, components, or systems and investigate failures or difficulties to diagnose operation faults.
Develop, coordinate, or monitor production activities, including selection of methods and fabrication processes.
Evaluate performance of equipment during testing and prepare technical reports for engineering and management use.
Investigate and observe tests on machinery for compliance with standards and implement operating methods or procedures to enhance efficiency.
Design and supervise testing, installation, and repair of machinery and equipment, ensuring compliance with specifications and safety standards.
Conduct educational programs that inform agricultural producers about improved productivity and provide advice on water quality and pollution management.
Design agricultural machinery components and equipment, and visit sites to monitor construction and environmental issues.
Conduct environmental and performance tests on agricultural and marine machinery and equipment.
Develop or evaluate systems or methods of storage, especially for fuel cell applications, and design new aerospace systems to enhance environmental performance.
Maintain records of engineering activities including performance reports, and serve as a liaison between clients and technical teams.
Conduct analytical studies to enhance the design of marine or aerospace products and ensure compliance with specifications and regulations.
Install doors, hatches, brackets, and clips.
Prepare or present technical or project status reports.
Design or engineer filtration systems that reduce harmful emissions and evaluate biofuel performance specifications for aerospace applications.
Establish production or quality control standards and coordinate production activities with other functional units, such as procurement, maintenance, or quality control.
Read current literature, attend meetings or conferences, or talk with colleagues to stay abreast of new automotive technology or competitive products.
Design fuel cell systems, subsystems, stacks, and components, and conduct fuel cell testing or development programs, including analyzing test data using statistical software.
Calculate the efficiency or power output of a fuel cell system, characterize their performances, and plan or conduct experiments for validation.
Develop fuel cell materials or test equipment, manage fuel cell hybrid system architecture, and recommend system design improvements.
Fabricate prototypes of fuel cell components, assemblies, and systems, and conduct failure analyses using diagnostic principles.
Identify vehicle integration challenges and integrate electric drive subsystems with other vehicle systems for optimized performance.
Design control systems for automotive applications and carry out automotive design reviews.
Conduct system-level automotive testing and validate new materials or protocols.
Lay out, fabricate, and fit metal structural parts and read blueprints to assess specifications and determine tooling instructions.
Calculate layout dimensions and verify points for workpieces in preparation for machining operations.
Perform machining tasks such as sawing, cutting, and milling, and ensure conformance to specifications through measurement and inspection.
Operate and set up machines for various metalworking processes, observing operations to detect malfunctions and adjust parameters as needed.
Select, install, and maintain cutting tools and repairing or replacing machine parts as necessary.
Clean and lubricate machines, work areas, and tools to ensure a safe and effective working environment.
Perform minor assembly tasks, such as fastening parts with nuts, bolts, or screws.
Study blueprints, layouts, and charts to determine material requirements and operational sequences.
Operate and maintain renewable energy equipment and evaluate energy performance or environmental impacts in designs.
Research innovations in green automotive technologies and alternative fuels.
Direct the installation, operation, maintenance, or repair of renewable energy systems, and recommend utility or energy services that minimize carbon footprints.
Preheat workpieces, using heating furnaces or hand torches.
Thread and hand-feed materials through machine cutters or abraders.
Compute machine indexings and settings for specified dimensions and base reference points.
Brush or spray lubricating compounds on workpieces, or turn valve handles and direct flow of coolant against tools and workpieces.
Move machine controls to index workpieces, and adjust machines for pre-selected operational settings.
Activate machine start-up switches to grind, lap, hone, debar, shear, or cut workpieces according to specifications.
Mount and position tools in machine chucks, spindles, or other tool holding devices, using hand tools.
Study blueprints, work orders, or machining instructions to determine product specifications, tool requirements, and operational sequences.
Select machine tooling to be used based on knowledge of machine and production requirements.
Measure workpieces and lay out work using precision measuring devices.
Set and adjust machine controls according to product specifications using knowledge of machine operation.
Observe machine operations to detect problems, making necessary adjustments to correct issues.
Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces.
Lift and position workpieces, manually or with hoists, and secure them in hoppers or on machine tables, faceplates, or chucks using clamps.
Inspect or measure finished workpieces to determine conformance to specifications using measuring instruments such as gauges or micrometers.
Refill, change, and monitor the level of fluids such as oil and coolant in machines.
Install tools in spindles.
Select and set cutting speeds, feed rates, depths of cuts, and cutting tools according to machining instructions or knowledge of metal properties.
Select, clean, and install spacers, rubber sleeves, or cutters on arbors.
Operate forklifts to deliver materials.
Hone cutters with oilstones to remove nicks.
Remove housings, feed tubes, tool holders, or other accessories to replace worn or broken parts such as springs or bushings.
Sharpen dulled blades using bench grinders, abrasive wheels, or lathes.
Grind out burrs or sharp edges using portable grinders, speed lathes, or polishing jacks.
Reline or repair ladles and pouring spouts with refractory clay, using trowels.
Chip slag from linings of ladles or remove linings when beyond repair using hammers and chisels.
Mix specified amounts of sand, clay, mortar powder, and water to form refractory clay or mortar, using shovels or mixing machines.
Activate shears and grinders to trim workpieces.
Read rolling orders, blueprints, and mill schedules to determine setup specifications, work sequences, product dimensions, and installation procedures.
Manipulate controls and observe dial indicators to monitor, adjust, and regulate speeds of machine mechanisms.
Start operation of rolling and milling machines to flatten, temper, form, and reduce sheet metal sections and to produce steel strips.
Set distance points between rolls, guides, meters, and stops according to specifications.
Signal and assist other workers to remove and position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines.
Calculate draft space and roll speed for each mill stand to plan rolling sequences and specified dimensions and tempers.
Monitor machine cycles and mill operation to detect jamming and to ensure that products conform to specifications.
Install equipment such as guides, guards, gears, cooling equipment, and rolls, using hand tools.
Record mill production on schedule sheets.
Fill oil cups, adjust valves, and observe gauges to control flow of metal coolants and lubricants onto workpieces.
Direct and train other workers to change rolls, operate mill equipment, remove coils and cobbles, and band and load material.
Position, align, and secure arbors, spindles, coils, mandrels, dies, and slitting knives.
Examine, inspect, and measure raw materials and finished products to verify conformance to specifications.
Adjust and correct machine setups to reduce thicknesses, reshape products, and eliminate defects.
Lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes.
Perform preventative maintenance or minor repairs on machines.
Clean work areas and maintain clean working environments according to clean room standards.
Monitor inventory levels and order supplies as necessary.

Adjust or maintain equipment such as lasers, laser systems, microscopes, oscilloscopes, pulse generators, power meters, beam analyzers, or energy measurement devices, and repair or calibrate products like surgical lasers.

Assemble fiber optical, optoelectronic, or free-space optics components, subcomponents, assemblies, or subassemblies, and assemble or adjust parts or related electrical units of prototypes to prepare for testing.

Conduct tests using prototype or test apparatus like control consoles, collimators, recording equipment, or cables, and develop or use new non-destructive testing methods such as acoustic emission testing, leak testing, and thermal or infrared testing.

Visually examine materials, structures, or components for signs of corrosion, metal fatigue, cracks, or other flaws, and make radiographic images to detect flaws in objects while leaving objects intact.
Map the presence of imperfections within objects using sonic measurements, and produce images of objects on film using radiographic techniques.

Supervise or direct the work of non-destructive testing trainees or staff; document non-destructive testing methods, processes, or results; and recommend or initiate personnel actions such as hires, promotions, or disciplinary measures.

Counsel employees about work-related issues and assist them to correct job-skill deficiencies; investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings; and confer with personnel to coordinate work activities or resolve employee grievances.
Compute estimates and actual costs of materials, labor, or outside contractors, and prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents.

Interpret specifications, blueprints, or job orders to construct templates and layout reference points for workers, and review orders, production schedules, blueprints, or shipping or receiving notices to determine work sequences.
Conduct or arrange worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use.

Inspect, test, and measure completed work using hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements, and examine materials, stock, vehicles, or equipment to ensure safety and compliance with specifications.
Maintain or verify records of time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities, and monitor tool and part inventories and the maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions.
Requisition materials and supplies such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts, and examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements.
Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet operational or production goals, and determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities based on priority and personnel skill.
Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or resolve problems, and monitor employees' work levels and review work performance.
Enforce safety rules and regulations and inspect work areas to ensure conformance to established standards in cleanliness and maintenance.
Develop or implement maintenance policies and procedures; perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using equipment such as hand or power tools, hydraulic presses, or welding equipment.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials, and evaluate material properties using various measurements when applicable.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments.
Participate in budget preparation and administration, coordinating purchasing and monitoring departmental expenditures.
Develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or computer information management systems, and design equipment configurations to meet personnel needs.
Compile operational or personnel records such as time and production records or repair statistics.
Review, evaluate, accept, and coordinate completion of work bids from contractors.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials; and dispatch personnel and vehicles in response to emergencies.
